notes: Each record is one line with flat space-separated key=value tokens.
The line begins with named fields rather than a free-text header; treat the first fields as ordinary payload keys, not a syslog-style prefix.
Timestamp is carried in datetime=... using ISO8601 Zulu format.
Event identity is best taken from explicit event=... field, not inferred from token position or casing.
Values are mostly unquoted single tokens; some fields may contain quoted strings with spaces, e.g. msg="..." and action="...".
Underscores are used heavily in keys and values; preserve full token text.
Schema is sparse by event type: state transitions, recipe definitions, step lifecycle, sensor snapshots, alarms, and result summaries share the same flat key=value envelope.
Numeric measurements and counters appear as plain scalar values with unit-bearing key names, e.g. reactor_temp_C, chamber_press_Pa, pulse_A_ms.
A deterministic parser can be specified as: split records by newline, then parse space-delimited key=value pairs with quote-aware value handling.

data.action: Leak check performed, O-ring replaced at chamber lid
data.msg: Reactor temperature above upper warning limit

llm_raw_spec.family: single-line space-delimited key=value equipment/process event logs

generic_text_record.fallback_when: line contains parseable space-delimited key=value pairs but event value is absent or unmapped
